text: Abraham moved south and settled for a while between Kadesh and Shur, then stayed in Gerar.
He said Sarah was his sister, so King Abimelech of Gerar sent for her and took her.
God came to Abimelech in a dream and said he was as good as dead for taking a married woman.
Abimelech had not gone near her and protested, would God destroy an innocent nation?
He said Abraham and Sarah both told him they were siblings, and he had acted with a clear conscience.
God agreed he had acted innocently and said that was why he had kept him from sinning by not letting him touch her.
God told him to return Abraham's wife, since Abraham was a prophet who would pray for him; otherwise he and his household would die.
Abimelech rose early, told his servants everything, and they were terrified.
He summoned Abraham and asked what he had done to bring such guilt on his kingdom.
He asked Abraham what he had been thinking to do this.
Abraham said he assumed there was no fear of God here and that they would kill him for his wife.
He explained Sarah really was his half-sister, his father's daughter but not his mother's, and so became his wife.
He said that whenever they traveled, he'd asked Sarah to call him her brother as a kindness.
Abimelech gave Abraham sheep, oxen, and servants, and returned Sarah to him.
Abimelech told Abraham his land was open to him to settle wherever he pleased.
To Sarah he gave a thousand pieces of silver as a public vindication before everyone.
Abraham prayed to God, and God healed Abimelech, his wife, and his slave women so they could bear children again.
The LORD had closed every womb in Abimelech's household because of Sarah, Abraham's wife.
